decks.notes: The main idea of this deck is to quickly assemble the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle combo to create a powerful threat that can't be dealt with easily. The deck includes a variety of counterspells and card draw spells to help find the combo pieces and protect them once they are on the battlefield. The Flash of Insight and Vision Charm cards also provide additional ways to manipulate the deck and find the necessary cards. The strategy focuses on disrupting the opponent while setting up the combo for a decisive victory.

This premodern combo deck revolves around using Hermit Druid to fill the graveyard with creatures and then reanimating them with cards like Reanimate and Animate Dead. By combining cards like Phyrexian Devourer and Triskelion, the deck can create a lethal combo to win the game. Cabal Therapy is used to disrupt the opponent's strategy and ensure the combo can go off smoothly. With the right sequencing and resource management, this deck can quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures.

The main concept of this deck is to control the game by utilizing counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to maintain board presence while slowing down the opponent's game plan. The deck also focuses on disrupting the opponent by using cards like Humility and Powder Keg to limit their options. Landstill is key to the strategy, allowing the player to draw cards and keep mana up for counterspells while potentially locking the opponent out of the game. The deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through incremental advantage.

This premade deck focuses on using powerful control tactics, utilizing cards like Pernicious Deed, Swords to Plowshares, and Cabal Therapy to disrupt the opponent's strategy. The deck also includes cards like Sylvan Library and Ravenous Baloth for card advantage and board presence. With a mix of removal, hand disruption, and creatures to apply pressure, this deck aims to outlast and outplay the opponent in a 1 vs 1 match. The inclusion of Living Wish adds versatility and additional toolbox options, enhancing the deck's adaptability in various situations.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and card draw to outvalue the opponent. The key strategy is to establish control of the board through cards like Wrath of God and Akroma's Vengeance, while also disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells like Counterspell. The deck aims to maintain card advantage through cards like Fact or Fiction and Accumulated Knowledge, while also having a win condition with cards like Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice. The deck also utilizes land cards effectively to generate mana and enable powerful plays.

This Aggro Goblin deck focuses on quickly summoning and buffing up a strong army of Goblins to overwhelm the opponent. By using cards like Goblin Lackey, Goblin Ringleader, and Goblin Piledriver, along with the mana ramp from Gaea's Cradle and Birds of Paradise, the deck aims to swarm the battlefield and deal damage fast. Survival of the Fittest allows for deck thinning and consistency, while other cards like Mogg Fanatic and Siege-Gang Commander provide additional removal and reach. The deck aims to win quickly and decisively before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly overwhelm the opponent with a horde of aggressive goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Matron and Goblin Lackey to fetch and put them into play. By utilizing cards such as Goblin Ringleader and Goblin Warchief, the deck aims to maintain card advantage and reduce the cost of goblin spells, allowing for a fast and powerful assault on the opponent. The inclusion of removal spells like Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter helps to clear the way for the goblin army to push through and secure victory.

The goal of this deck is to consistently and efficiently combo off using the Phyrexian Dreadnought and Stifle interaction. By playing cards like Gush, Portent, and Accumulated Knowledge, the deck aims to draw into its combo pieces while also disrupting the opponent with Counterspells and card draw. The inclusion of cards like Daze and Foil further enhance the deck's ability to protect its combo while maintaining card advantage. With careful planning and timing, the deck can quickly assemble its combo and overwhelm the opponent before they have a chance to react.

The deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal spells, and land destruction to disrupt the opponent's game plan. It also utilizes creatures with disruptive abilities like Meddling Mage and Whipcorder to further control the board. The deck aims to stall the game with cards like Standstill and Weathered Wayfarer until it can establish a dominant position with powerful threats like Exalted Angel. The combination of control elements and late-game threats provides a versatile approach to dealing with a variety of opposing strategies.

The main concept of this deck is to create a powerful combo using enchantments, specifically Argothian Enchantress, Enchantress's Presence, and Sterling Grove to draw cards and generate mana. By utilizing cards like Opalescence and Parallax Wave, the deck aims to control the board while setting up for a big finish with Enchantress synergy. The deck includes utility cards like Seal of Cleansing and Swords to Plowshares for removal, as well as Serra's Sanctum for additional mana acceleration. With the right setup and timing, this deck can overwhelm opponents with a flurry of enchantment-based combos.

The main focus of this deck is to create a powerful enchantment-based combo using cards like Opalescence, Serra's Sanctum, and Enchantress's Presence. By ramping up mana with Wild Growth and Sterling Grove, the player can quickly set up a strong board presence with Argothian Enchantress and other enchantments. Using cards like Solitary Confinement and Parallax Wave, the deck aims to control the board while drawing cards and gaining advantage. The inclusion of Replenish allows for a massive board recovery if necessary, creating a powerful and resilient combo deck.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through cards like Mox Diamond, Counterspell, and Swords to Plowshares, while also utilizing land denial strategies with cards like Tsabo's Web and Armageddon. The deck aims to out-value the opponent through powerful card draw options like Deep Analysis and Fact or Fiction, and has ways to shuffle important cards back into the library with Gaea's Blessing. The combination of land control, card advantage, and powerful removal spells allows this deck to dictate the pace of the game and outmaneuver the opponent.

The main concept of this deck is to utilize the combo involving Hermit Druid, which mills the entire library and then reanimates a creature like Karmic Guide or Triskelion to win the game. The deck includes various cards to support the combo such as Unearth, Survival of the Fittest, and Volrath's Shapeshifter. Additionally, there are disruption elements like Cabal Therapy and Duress to protect the combo and control the opponent's hand. The deck aims to efficiently and quickly assemble the combo pieces to achieve victory.

The deck is focused on overwhelming the opponent with fast and aggressive plays, using low-cost burn spells and efficient creatures to deal damage quickly. It aims to utilize the synergy between cards like Grim Lavamancer, Flame Rift, and Seal of Fire to apply constant pressure, while also leveraging Price of Progress as a potent finisher. By maintaining a relentless and proactive strategy, the deck aims to secure victory before the opponent has a chance to stabilize or mount a defense.

This deck focuses on controlling the game by using counterspells, removal, and land denial to slow down the opponent's strategy. It aims to maintain a stable board state through cards like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God while generating card advantage with Fact or Fiction. The deck also includes win conditions like Decree of Justice and Exalted Angel to close out the game when the time is right. Overall, this archetype excels at controlling the pace of the game and disrupting opponent's plans while setting up its own win conditions.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by using various counterspells, removal spells, and card advantage cards. The deck aims to stall the game with cards like Standstill and Humility while using Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave as creature threats. The deck also includes land destruction elements like Dust Bowl to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the deck's strategy revolves around maintaining control of the game through a combination of counter magic, removal, and card advantage to outvalue and outlast the opponent.
